mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and Proxy Server designed to facilitate interactions with over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It provides features such as cost tracking, load balancing, and logging, making it ideal for applications requiring diverse LLM integrations. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for proper resource allocation to handle multiple API calls efficiently.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5

kubectl version --client

helm version

```

> Install the LiteLLM Python SDK.
> Verify that kubectl is installed.
> Verify that Helm is installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Update kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Ensure all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install LiteLLM Proxy Server
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM Proxy Server in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-service.yaml

```

> Deploy LiteLLM using the provided YAML.
> Expose the LiteLLM service.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-monitoring.yaml

```

> Enable autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Deploy monitoring for LiteLLM.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the deployment.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-service.yaml

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current deployment and configurations. After upgrading, verify that the deployment is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get deployment litellm -o yaml > litellm-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/litellm

```

> Backup current deployment configuration.
> Apply the new deployment configuration.
> Verify that the deployment has been successfully updated.
commands: kubectl get deployment litellm -o yaml > litellm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is incorrectly tagged.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in the deployment YAML file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource limits as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM service may not be properly exposed or the deployment is down.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the deployment is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** API keys or environment variables may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that all required API keys are set in the environment variables.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLMs and improved API response handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
